data.title: Gold tumbles after Trump unveils U.K. trade deal, boosting hopes for progress with China
data.description:
Gold futures fell sharply in volatile trading on Thursday after President Trump announced a trade deal with the U.K., raising hopes of such deals with other countries, and with initial talks inolving U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer set to meet with China's top economic official this weekend in Switzerland.

Trump and British Prime Minister Starmer announced a "breakthrough deal" on trade, which will maintain a 10% tariff on goods imported from the U.K. while Britain agreed to lower its tariffs to 1.8% from 5.1% and provide greater access to U.S. goods.

U.S. stock indexes have rallied as risk sentiment has returned to the markets, which has weighed on gold's safe-haven appeal, but analysts warn that while the market is pricing in some optimism for a China deal, the talks could be long and drawn-out, limiting the upside potential for risk assets and gold's slide.

Elsewhere, China's central bank approved foreign exchange purchases by commercial banks to pay for gold imports under recently increased quotas, Reuters reported.

"Theoretically, this move should boost gold prices as increased demand from China becomes a factor. However, current market dynamics are dominated by developments surrounding tariffs," MarketPulse by OANDA analyst Zain Vawda said in response to the report.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for May delivery closed -2.5% to $3,296.60/oz, and front-month May Comex silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended -0.4% to $32.378/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Axalta Coating Systems downgraded at J.P. Morgan on cautious outlook
data.description:
Axalta Coating Systems (NYSE:AXTA) on Thursday was downgraded to Neutral from a previous investment rating of Overweight by analysts at financial-services firm J.P. Morgan. They pointed to the company’s tempered second-quarter guidance and concerns about industry challenges.

“Axalta is cutting costs,” Jeff Zekauskas, research analyst at J.P. Morgan, said a May 8 note to clients. “Axalta’s restructuring efforts led to a good first-quarter 2025 earnings performance with both costs of goods sold, SG&A and R&D values falling year over year.”

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization grew 4% in the quarter, while adjusted earnings per share rose 16%, aided by lower interest expenses.

Volume pressures

Despite the profit gains, total volumes declined by a few percentage points year-over-year -- a trend Zekauskas expects will persist for the rest of 2025. Nonetheless, he said he believes Axalta’s (NYSE:AXTA) earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization can still rise 3% this year, and EPS may grow 9%, from $2.35 to $2.55 per share.

Axalta (AXTA) maintained its full-year ebitda guidance of $1.15 billion to $1.175 billion, even as it issued a more cautious forecast for the second quarter. The company now expects second-quarter ebitda to come in between $280 million and $290 million, below the pre-earnings consensus of $295 million.

“Normally there is a stronger, sequential, expected second quarter lift in ebitda,” Zekauskas noted, suggesting that “volume weakness and slower price initiatives in the auto Refinish market may be the factors at work.”

Weak auto production

Zekauskas cited concerns about weak global auto production, shrinking demand in the refinishing segment and soft industrial coatings markets as key reasons for the downgrade. He also flagged tariff-related cyclical risks and broader macroeconomic uncertainty as barriers to further valuation expansion.

“We do not find the risk/reward ratio of Axalta appealing at the current price,” he wrote, adding that under recessionary conditions, “it would take years, as a base case, for its earnings to rise to current levels.”

J.P.Morgan also lowered its price target for Axalta (AXTA) shares from $42 to $32, reflecting a more muted outlook and removing previous assumptions about multiple expansion. The valuation estimates an enterprise value that’s 8.5 times ebitda for 2025.

Pair-trade positioning

Zekauskas additionally signaled a shift in relative positioning within the coatings sector, commenting that “the long Axalta (AXTA)-short PPG Industries (PPG) pair is not as convincing a pair as it once was,” as valuation gaps have narrowed and PPG’s restructuring may offer more stability ahead.

data.title: nLight Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.04 beats by $0.15, revenue of $51.67M beats by $4.36M
data.description:
• nLight press release (NASDAQ:LASR): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.04 beats by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $51.67M (+16.0% Y/Y) beats by $4.36M.
  • Shares +12.89% AH.
  • For the second quarter of 2025, nLIGHT expects revenues to be in the range of $53 million to $59 million. The midpoint of $56 million includes Products revenue of approximately $38 million and Advanced Development revenue of approximately $18 million. nLIGHT expects overall gross margin to be in the range of 19% to 25%, with Products gross margin in the range of 27% to 33% and Advanced Development gross margin of approximately 8%. nLIGHT expects Adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of ($4) million to $1 million.
  • Q2 revenue consensus of $50.16M

data.title: Shift4 announces pricing of offering of €680M of senior notes due 2033 and $550M tack-on offering of senior notes due 2032
data.description:
• Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR) on Thursday said its subsidiaries, Shift4 Payments and Shift4 Payments Finance Sub, have priced an offering of €680 million aggregate principal amount of 5.500% senior notes due 2033 and $550 million in aggregate principal amount of their 6.750% senior notes due 2032.
  • The new 2032 notes will be issued as additional securities under an Indenture, dated August 15, 2024, pursuant to which the issuers issued $1,100 million in aggregate principal amount of its 6.750% senior notes due 2032 in August 2024.
  • The new 2032 notes and the existing 2032 notes are intended to be treated as a single class of debt securities under the 2032 notes indenture, and the new 2032 notes will have identical terms to the existing 2032 notes, other than with respect to the issue date and issue price.
  • The Euro notes were priced at par value and will bear an interest rate of 5.500% per annum. The new 2032 notes were priced at an issue price of 100.50% and will bear an interest rate of 6.750% per annum.
  • Shift4 expects to close the offering of the Notes on May 16, 2025.
  • The issuers expect to redeem or repay the 2026 notes after the completion of the offering. However, there can be no assurances that the 2026 notes will be redeemed or repaid.
  • Source: Press Release
